_meta.label: Terra Liquidity Alliance (TLA)
_meta.summary: TLA (the Alliance Liquidity Hub, operated by Eris Protocol) is the core primitive this whole project exists to track. A ve(3,3) system that converts Terra chain staking rewards into vote-directed liquidity incentives, building permanent chain-owned liquidity. Weekly epochs. Mechanism facts sourced to the Eris Liquidity Hub doc; practice/behavior facts sourced to our own verified research (PROJECT_KNOWLEDGE).

facts.claim: The Alliance Liquidity Hub (TLA) is Terra's central liquidity-incentive engine: it converts chain staking rewards into incentives for liquidity providers and voters, creating a positive liquidity feedback loop. It is a ve(3,3)-style system, but instead of token emissions it distributes chain staking rewards.
TLA operates on weekly epochs ending Sunday 23:59 UTC (Monday 00:00 UTC). Votes, incentives, and reward distributions are organized per epoch. The canonical epoch schedule is 1-indexed.
facts.notes: Epoch end time and 1-indexed schedule confirmed in our PROJECT_KNOWLEDGE; canonical schedule lives in epoch_1-300_date.json.

facts.claim: Voters lock LUNA, ampLUNA, or bLUNA to create NFT-based locks (CW721). Locks grant voting power scaled by lock time: a 1-week lock = factor 1, a max 2-year lock = factor 10. Locks can be auto-max-locked to permanently hold VP at 10x, and being NFTs can be used as collateral.

Voting power always counts the underlying LUNA amount as the fixed base. For LUNA it equals the lock amount; for LSTs it is lock_amount × LST redemption rate — so it is beneficial to lock with LSTs. This is why the LST hub redemption rates we compute feed directly into VP math.
facts.notes: Direct link between our LST redemption work and TLA VP — the redemption rate is not just a price input, it sizes voting power.

facts.claim: Voters decide how liquidity rewards are distributed across LP asset groups through gauge voting. The reward allocation per LP group each epoch is determined by where voting power is directed.
TLA organizes pools into four buckets, each with its own separate reward allocation: Stable, Project, Bluechip, and Single. Each user's voting power allocates once per bucket (4 buckets).
facts.notes: Bucket contract addresses are in platform-crons config/contracts.js (STAKING_BUCKETS). Per-bucket allocation is why pool-summed VP inflates 4x — use max bucket VP as canonical Total TLA VP.

facts.claim: Liquidity incentives: Alliance chain staking rewards are converted and distributed as z-assets to liquidity providers. The distribution across LP groups is decided by voters. This is the LP emissions stream (pending_rewards).

Voting incentives (bribes): each epoch, projects and users can add incentives so voters are motivated to vote for their LPs. These go to VOTERS (pending_bribes), a separate stream from LP emissions. Phoenix Directive is a regular bribe provider.

Rebase: additional rewards to voters based on the underlying LUNA amount they locked. Because liquidity incentives are distributed as z-assets, the yield those z-assets generate (via liquid staking) flows back to voters as rebase.

Bribes (voting incentives) go to VOTERS, not to LP depositors. LP depositors receive emissions (liquidity incentives). These are separate streams — a critical distinction: realized LP yield should NOT include bribes.
facts.notes: Hard-won correction: an early pass wrongly read realized ~86% vs advertised ~50% as 'bribes paid on top.' Truth: bribes are a separate voter stream. Like-for-like, TLA pays ≈ what it advertises (amp ~59% vs ~63% advertised; non-amp ~49% vs ~51%), marginally under — the drag is the compounder reward fee, not bribe leakage.

facts.claim: The chain shifts incentives from staking to liquidity via an LP-specific take rate (~10%): a portion of staked-LP value goes to the Community Pool, which acquires permanent chain-owned liquidity (Protocol-Owned Liquidity / PoL). This builds a lasting deep-liquidity economy.
facts.notes: 10% take rate figure confirmed in our PROJECT_KNOWLEDGE and tla_docs_content glossary.

facts.claim: The DAO's TLA success metric is ampLP token COUNT growth per epoch — the goal each epoch is to gain ampLP tokens, not lose them. USD value can fluctuate with the market; what matters is the underlying ampLP count rising. Dashboards should surface count growth, not just a USD figure.

LP positions are staked into TLA in four variants: Zap (any token, auto-swapped to LP ratio), Tokens (use the pool's own tokens), LP (stake an existing LP), or Staked (transfer an Astroport-incentive-staked LP directly). Rewards are claimable in ampLUNA, bLUNA, or LUNA.

LPs can be staked amplified (auto-compounding via Eris Amp Compounder → ampLP) or non-amplified. Amplified realized yield is an APY (auto-compounds) vs advertised APR; like-for-like the amp pool runs marginally under advertised, the gap being the compounder reward fee.
facts.notes: Cross-references eris amplp facts. APR-vs-APY distinction was a source of an early misread, now corrected.

facts.claim: aDAO NFTs hold an 'Ally' token earning ~0.72% LUNA staking rewards via Terra Alliance, with a ~40% boost via Eris ampLUNA conversion, and a 10% take rate to the DAO Treasury. This is the aDAO-specific yield path into the TLA economy.

At the TLA-wide level, total VP must be counted carefully: each user's VP allocates once per bucket across 4 buckets, so pool-summed VP inflates 4x. Use max bucket VP as canonical Total TLA VP (matches the Eris UI). Lock events also carry a canonical flag — VP/lock-delta math must filter canonical===true to avoid double-counting wrapper-layer events.

Core TLA contracts on phoenix-1: Gauge Controller (gauge voting), Voting Escrow / vAMP minter (lock NFTs, VP), Incentive Manager (bribes), and four staking buckets. These are the structural source of truth in platform-crons config/contracts.js.
facts.notes: Originally discovered via HAR capture of the Eris liquidity-hub Vote tab fetcher (May 2026).

facts.claim: The TLA contracts are open-source at github.com/erisprotocol/contracts-ve3: asset-staking (Alliance Protocol staking — distributes rewards via take rates and bribes), voting-escrow (lock tokens for governance/delegation power, boosted by lock duration, managed via an NFT collection), and global-config. This is the authoritative source for the exact on-chain mechanism.
facts.notes: Repo + scope identified from the SCV ve3 audit (see tla.audit.scv_ve3). Auditor's own scope description independently corroborates the take-rate + bribe + lock-NFT mechanism we sourced from the Eris docs.

facts.claim: Eris commissioned an independent security audit of the TLA ve3 contracts by SCV Security (ERIS - Contracts ve3 - Audit Report v1.0, 1 August 2024), covering asset-staking, voting-escrow, and global-config at github.com/erisprotocol/contracts-ve3.
facts.notes: Public report. Readers can verify the TLA contract behavior against an independent third-party audit, not just project docs.

facts.claim: Eris commissioned an Oak Security audit (2023-02-15) of the Amplified Staking Hub contract and voting_escrow at github.com/erisprotocol/contracts-terra — the staking-hub mechanism behind ampLUNA's exchange rate.
facts.notes: Independent audit of the hub contract whose exchange_rate our cron queries for LST redemption pricing.

facts.claim: The current canonical Total TLA VP (max bucket VP).
facts.notes: Changes each epoch as locks are created/expire; never hardcode.
